vol_name: Self-directed learning by preschoolers in a naturalistic overhearing context
vol_description:
Experiment 1: 3-4.5-year-olds
    2 conditions: Overhearing (phone call; halfalogue), Pedagogical (direct instruction)
Experiment 2: 4.5-6-year-olds
    2 conditions: Overhearing (phone call; halfalogue), Pedagogical (direct instruction)
Experiment 3: 3-4.5-year-olds
    1 condition: Overhearing (phone call on speaker phone; dialogue)

This study assesses the role of polysemy in children's word extension performance and memory for novel words
Participants are children aged 4-6, and adults 
Videos of experimental sessions stored here
eyetracking during viewing of a picture book narrated at two different levels of linguistic complexity

referential communication task 
participants: 5-year-olds and adults
Contrasting linguistic complexity task composed of a standardized vocabulary measure (PPVT) and speaker habituation and preference.

This volume contains a variety of data from each participant, including:
- Videos from an in-lab selective attention task
- Eyetracking data during selective attention task
- Videos from an at-home overhearing task
- LENA summary files
keynote presentation with script presenting disagreements over 7 adjectives, grounded in experience or perspective (within-subjects)
This volume contains video and audio files from data collected in India during Wave 1 of the project.

This volume stores task-by-task subsets of DBN data collection in India (Hindu & Muslim). This includes data from the Child Protocol, Parent Protocol, and Religious Landscape Survey. These data and the volume are to be kept PRIVATE!
